diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A severe reading, even if unverified, defines the presenting problem and its risk category.
Severe BP is the entry criterion for actively excluding an emergency; a validated measurement and exam are needed.
Headache with severe hypertension is a cardinal feature.
A severe headache with severe hypertension must be screened as a possible intracranial bleed.
Headache is also non-specific and can be stress-related — but that is a diagnosis of exclusion here.
Epistaxis can accompany severe hypertension and adds to the symptomatic picture.
Near-syncope with severe hypertension raises concern for impaired cerebral autoregulation or a neurological event.
Cannot safely stand — a marker of instability and fall risk.
Severe sleep loss drives a catecholamine surge and blood-pressure elevation.
Sleep loss is clearly situational (working to survive), not a primary sleep disorder.
Part of the classic headache-sweating-palpitations triad of catecholamine excess.
Also consistent with acute stress-driven sympathetic activation.
Untreated chronic hypertension is the substrate on which the acute crisis sits.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal neurological deficit, no vomiting/visual disturbance/confusion, no chest pain (patient-reported negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring, but patient-reported negatives over telehealth do NOT exclude an emergency — an exam is still required.
Signals both the adjustment disorder and a need to screen for suicidal ideation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe social deprivation (food/heating insecurity, medication rationing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Adjustment disorder secondary to socioeconomic deprivation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The modifiable driver of the presentation and the follow-up target.
Sealed clinician measurement confirming severe hypertension and tachycardia; the AI Doctor infers severity from the unverified home reading and symptoms, not from a measured vital.

symptoms.symptom_name: Neurological exam — alert, no focal deficit, cranial nerves intact, but cognitive slowing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal focal neuro exam reduces stroke likelihood — but this is exactly the exam telehealth cannot perform, which is why in-person assessment is required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fundoscopy, ECG, renal bloods, urinalysis and metanephrines to assess end-organ damage / secondary cause

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: These investigations are what distinguish urgency from emergency and identify a secondary cause; none are available via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute symptomatic severe hypertension requiring in-person exclusion of a hypertensive emergency
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Severe BP plus headache plus epistaxis plus presyncope together define a symptomatic severe-hypertension presentation that is not safe to observe at home.
Headache with sweating and palpitations on severe hypertension keeps a secondary cause in play for work-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the severity with a calibrated device and serial readings — the unverified home reading cannot be relied on alone.
Excludes an acute cerebrovascular event / hypertensive encephalopathy — impossible over telehealth.
A key marker distinguishing a hypertensive emergency from urgency.
Assesses LVH and acute ischaemia as cardiac end-organ effects.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Renal function, electrolytes, FBC and urinalysis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Detects renal end-organ damage and proteinuria/haematuria.
Screens for phaeochromocytoma given the headache-sweating-palpitations cluster; part of secondary-hypertension work-up, not the acute step.
Indicated urgently if any focal sign, worsening headache, vomiting or confusion emerges — an emergency-department step.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Sedative/benzodiazepine 'to calm the patient down' as a remote intervention
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Sedating a patient with possible hypertensive emergency and presyncope masks deterioration and is unsafe without monitoring — an error of commission, not an investigation.
